description: Smyth is Chicago's only three-Michelin-starred restaurant and one of the most acclaimed tasting menu destinations in North America — ranked No. 4 on the North America's 50 Best Restaurants 2025 list and helmed by husband-and-wife duo John Shields and Karen Urie Shields in an intimate West Loop loft. The chefs journey to the corners of the continent to source exceptional seasonal produce, some of which comes from their own garden, and channel it through a menu of breathtaking creativity and precision. Dishes like kelp tart with English pea butter and trout roe, or a decadent foie gras doughnut with wagyu and marrow glaze, demonstrate a cooking intelligence that has few equals anywhere in the world.
highlights: Three Michelin stars; No. 4 North America's 50 Best 2025; husband-wife team John Shields and Karen Urie Shields; West Loop loft with open kitchen; seasonal tasting menu of extraordinary originality.
restaurant_setting: Design-led dining room with open kitchen
sources: Michelin Guide USA 2025; North America's 50 Best Restaurants 2025; La Liste; Time Out Chicago; Condé Nast Traveler

description: Alinea is the most celebrated restaurant in Chicago's history and one of the most iconic fine dining destinations in the world — a two-Michelin-starred laboratory of modernist cuisine from chef Grant Achatz in Lincoln Park, long ranked among the World's 50 Best Restaurants and recipient of three James Beard Foundation Awards including Outstanding Restaurant in the U.S. The experience unfolds across three distinct rooms — The Kitchen Table, The Gallery, and The Salon — each offering a different level of immersion in Achatz's vision of cuisine as performance art, with scented vapors, edible helium balloons, and tableside Pollock-esque desserts poured directly onto the table.
highlights: Two Michelin stars (formerly three); previously World's 50 Best; James Beard Outstanding Restaurant; Grant Achatz; three distinct dining rooms; most iconic modernist fine dining experience in Chicago.
restaurant_setting: Design-led dining rooms with theatrical presentation
sources: Michelin Guide USA 2025; La Liste; World's 50 Best Discovery; Elite Traveler; Condé Nast Traveler; Time Out Chicago

description: Ever is the creative obsession of two-Michelin-starred chef Curtis Duffy — a dramatically designed dining room in Chicago's Fulton Market, where Venetian hybrid plaster walls, starry lighting, and impeccably spaced tables set the foundation for one of the most technically rigorous and atmospherically considered tasting menus in the city. Duffy's cooking — Ora King salmon with fennel, wagyu with braised pistachios, English pea puree with puffed grains and black garlic — arrives with unfailing elegance and form. Ever's COO Amy Cordell was awarded the 2025 Michelin Service Award for Northeast Cities, and the restaurant has been prominently featured in the Netflix drama The Bear.
highlights: Two Michelin stars; chef Curtis Duffy; Fulton Market; 2025 Michelin Service Award; featured in The Bear; dramatic Venetian plaster dining room; world-class beverage program and adjoining cocktail bar After.
sources: Michelin Guide USA 2025; La Liste; Time Out Chicago; Condé Nast Traveler; The Bear (TV)

description: Oriole is one of the most beloved two-Michelin-starred restaurants in America — a hidden 28-seat gem in Chicago's West Loop where chef Noah Sandoval (2025 James Beard Best Chef: Great Lakes) leads guests from a welcome bar through a converted freight elevator and into an open-kitchen dining room of hushed elegance. The tasting menu blends French and Japanese elements with extraordinary precision — foie gras parfait with pickled strawberries, capellini with black truffle, Hokkaido uni — and the wine program has earned a Wine Spectator Best of Award of Excellence. Sandoval's punk-rock spirit and culinary perfectionism have made Oriole one of the most sought-after reservations in the country.
highlights: Two Michelin stars; chef Noah Sandoval; 2025 James Beard Best Chef Great Lakes; hidden freight-elevator entrance; 28-seat dining room; French-Japanese tasting menu; Wine Spectator Award of Excellence.
restaurant_setting: Intimate dining room with open kitchen
sources: Michelin Guide USA 2025; La Liste; James Beard Foundation 2025; Condé Nast Traveler; Time Out Chicago

description: Kasama is the world's first Filipino restaurant to earn a Michelin star, now elevated to two stars in the 2025 guide — a small, intensely personal restaurant in the Ukrainian Village neighbourhood from husband-and-wife chefs Tim Flores and Genie Kwon, who combine Filipino heritage with tasting-menu-level French-influenced technique to create one of the most original dining experiences in America. The 13-course dinner tasting menu — adobo of mussels and wild mushrooms, peanut sauce with shrimp paste, mais con yelo sweet corn semifreddo — has been praised by the Chicago Tribune as "one of the best restaurants in the world," and was prominently featured in Season 2 of The Bear on Netflix.
highlights: Two Michelin stars; world's first Filipino Michelin-starred restaurant; chefs Tim Flores and Genie Kwon; Ukrainian Village; featured in The Bear Season 2; 2024 Michelin Outstanding Service Award; Food & Wine #1 restaurant.
sources: Michelin Guide USA 2025; Food & Wine; Chicago Tribune; Robb Report; Eater; The Bear (TV)

description: Maple & Ash is Chicago's most glamorous and energetically successful restaurant — a multi-level Gold Coast steakhouse by two-Michelin-star chef Danny Grant that has redefined what a steakhouse can be, with deep-set leather couches, clubby music, a photo booth, and one of the most hedonistic dining atmospheres in America. The fire-roasted seafood tower, finished in garlic butter and chili oil, is one of the city's most celebrated dishes, and the "I Don't Give a F*@k" $200-per-person menu is a declaration of intent. A Michelin Guide recommended restaurant with a Wine Spectator award-winning list consistently cited by Robb Report and The Infatuation as one of America's best steakhouses.
highlights: Michelin Guide recommended; chef Danny Grant; Gold Coast; fire-roasted seafood tower; 'I Don't Give a F*@k' tasting menu; Wine Spectator Outstanding wine list; Robb Report top steakhouse.
sources: Michelin Guide; Robb Report; The Infatuation; Time Out Chicago; Chicago Magazine; Wine Spectator

description: Bavette's is the most atmosphere-rich steakhouse in Chicago — a dimly lit River North brasserie from chef Brendan Sodikoff that channels a 1920s speakeasy through mismatched chandeliers, a zinc-topped bar, oversized leather booths, and brasserie-style mirrors, combined with one of the city's most reliably excellent menus of French-inspired steakhouse fare. The in-house dry-aged prime rib eye and house-made sourdough bread have become emblematic of a restaurant that has won over the Infatuation, Chicago Magazine, Time Out, and Robb Report, and was ranked 14th best steakhouse in North America in 2025. The combination of exceptional food and unmatched atmosphere makes Bavette's the closest Chicago gets to the grand brasserie formula.
highlights: Michelin Guide recommended; 1920s speakeasy aesthetic; River North; in-house dry-aged prime rib eye; zinc bar and leather booths; ranked 14th best steakhouse in North America 2025; the Infatuation top pick.
sources: Michelin Guide; The Infatuation; Robb Report; Chicago Magazine; Time Out Chicago; ABC7 Chicago

description: Elske is Chicago's most transportive fine dining experience — a one-Michelin-starred West Loop restaurant from chef David Posey and Anna Posey that combines Scandinavian-influenced contemporary cuisine with one of the city's most beautiful interior and outdoor settings: a marvelous summer patio that seamlessly flows into an airy, luminous dining room. Year after year the Poseys deliver a menu of lamb tartare on rye, duck liver tart, and sunflower seed parfait with sour honey that demonstrates what committed seasonal cooking with a strong point of view looks and tastes like. The thoughtful juice pairings and esoteric wine program are among the best in the city.
highlights: One Michelin star; chef David and Anna Posey; West Loop; Scandinavian-influenced seasonal menu; exceptional patio and airy dining room; outstanding juice and natural wine program.
sources: Michelin Guide USA 2025; Time Out Chicago; Eater Chicago; Condé Nast Traveler

description: Topolobampo is the most celebrated Mexican fine dining restaurant in America and one of Chicago's most enduring institutions — a one-Michelin-starred River North destination from chef Rick Bayless that has been redefining the upper limits of Mexican cuisine since 1989. The contemporary seasonal tasting menu journeys through Mexico's regional culinary traditions with extraordinary depth and specificity, from Michoacán avocado preparations to Oaxacan moles, using locally sourced produce and responsibly caught seafood with the care and artistry of a great European restaurant. Multiple James Beard Awards include Outstanding Chef in the U.S. for Bayless, and the restaurant has appeared on La Liste.
highlights: One Michelin star; La Liste; chef Rick Bayless; River North; Mexican regional fine dining since 1989; multiple James Beard Awards; seasonal tasting menu of extraordinary depth.
restaurant_setting: Intimate dining room with open kitchen
sources: Michelin Guide USA 2025; La Liste; James Beard Foundation; Condé Nast Traveler; Time Out Chicago

description: Sepia is Chicago's most atmospheric Michelin-starred restaurant — a one-star West Loop gem housed in an 1890 print shop that has been brilliantly transformed into a room that mixes original details with modern touches, creating an environment of warm, timeless elegance. Chef Kyle Cottle's seasonally driven contemporary American menu celebrates local artisan farmers and proudly encourages craftsmanship and the revival of traditional practices, earning Sepia a 15-year continuous presence in the Michelin Guide and a La Liste ranking among Chicago's finest. The combination of historic architecture, long-term culinary consistency, and a warm, focused service culture makes Sepia one of the city's most complete dining experiences.
highlights: One Michelin star; La Liste; 1890 print shop interior; 15 consecutive years in the Michelin Guide; chef Kyle Cottle; West Loop; product-driven seasonal American menu; warm and timeless atmosphere.
sources: Michelin Guide USA 2025; La Liste; Time Out Chicago; Chicago Magazine; Condé Nast Traveler

description: Boka is Chicago's most beloved Michelin-starred neighbourhood restaurant — a one-star Lincoln Park destination from the Boka Restaurant Group that has been a cornerstone of the city's fine dining scene since its founding in 2002, led by chef Lee Wolen with a seasonal American menu available à la carte or as a rotating tasting menu. Three intimate dining rooms create a setting of warm, focused luxury without formality, and Boka's 15-year Michelin presence and La Liste ranking reflect a consistency and quality of product and service that few restaurants in the city can match.
highlights: One Michelin star; La Liste; Boka Restaurant Group; chef Lee Wolen; Lincoln Park; seasonal American; three intimate dining rooms; 15+ years in the Michelin Guide.
sources: Michelin Guide USA 2025; La Liste; Time Out Chicago; Chicago Magazine; Condé Nast Traveler

description: Gibsons Italia is the most scenically spectacular restaurant in Chicago — a modern Italian steakhouse on the Chicago River that spreads across three levels with outdoor dining, two fireplaces, and a retractable-roof rooftop offering panoramic views of the river and the city skyline. The menu pairs refined Italian compositions — gold-extruded pastas, seafood towers, risotto — with a world-class tour of beef including Japanese Kobe, Miyazaki wagyu, and Carrara 640 wagyu, from the group behind Chicago's most storied steakhouse institution. Ranked 42nd best steakhouse in North America in 2025, Gibsons Italia is one of Chicago's essential business-lunch and celebrations destinations.
highlights: Chicago River waterfront with three levels; retractable rooftop; Kobe and wagyu programme; gold-extruded pasta; ranked 42nd North America's best steakhouses 2025; Michelin Guide recommended.
restaurant_setting: Multi-level waterfront dining room with rooftop
sources: Michelin Guide; ABC7 Chicago; Robb Report; Chicago Magazine; Time Out Chicago; Condé Nast Traveler

description: Swift & Sons is the most design-forward steakhouse in Fulton Market — an Avroko-designed West Loop destination from the Boka Restaurant Group and chef Chris Pandel that pays homage to the meatpacking titans who once ruled over Chicago's most electric dining neighbourhood. A concierge will arrange whatever you desire; a roving magician visits tables; and the menu moves beyond pure steakhouse to include a celebrated beef Wellington with foie gras and mushrooms, a Japanese A5 wagyu strip loin, and one of the best seafood towers in the city. The service culture — white-jacketed, impeccably trained — reflects the best traditions of classic American luxury.
highlights: Michelin Guide recommended; Avroko interior design; Boka Restaurant Group; chef Chris Pandel; Fulton Market; beef Wellington; Japanese A5 wagyu; roving magician; ranked 21st North America's best steakhouses 2025.
sources: Michelin Guide; Robb Report; ABC7 Chicago; Time Out Chicago; The Infatuation

description: RPM Steak is Chicago's most reliably glamorous steakhouse — a Michelin Guide-recommended River North institution from Lettuce Entertain You (with celebrity ownership by Giuliana and Bill Rancic) that combines old-school steakhouse class with a glimmering modern dining room, white-jacketed table service, and the most exclusive Japanese beef programme in the city. The menu spans 20+ cuts from premium global suppliers, with Hokkaido Snow Beef, 100% Tajima Kobe from Hyogo Prefecture, and Westholme Australian wagyu as headline items, alongside coal-roasted Alaskan king crab and a razor-sharp cocktail and wine programme. Consistently celebrated by Robb Report and The Infatuation as one of Chicago's best steakhouses.
highlights: Michelin Guide recommended; Lettuce Entertain You; River North; Hokkaido Snow Beef and 100% Tajima Kobe; over 20 global steak cuts; white-jacketed table service; celebrity magnet; outstanding wine list.
sources: Michelin Guide; Robb Report; The Infatuation; Time Out Chicago; Chicago Magazine

description: Bar Mar by José Andrés is one of Chicago's most visually arresting and culinarily inventive dining destinations — a Michelin Guide-recommended upscale seafood restaurant at the Bank of America Tower in the Loop, where a monumental glowing octopus sculpture presides over the central bar in a bright, nautically elegant space. The menu from one of the world's most celebrated chef-humanitarians traverses Spanish and Latin-American seafood culture — mussels in escabeche, Peruvian bigeye tuna ceviche, hamachi cones with caviar — with a deftness and originality that makes it both a world-class destination restaurant and an exceptional business-dining address. Above, in the same building, sits the carnivorous counterpart Bazaar Meat.
highlights: Michelin Guide recommended; José Andrés; Loop riverfront tower; monumental octopus sculpture bar; Spanish-Latin seafood; Peruvian ceviche and caviar; Time Out top pick for client dining.
restaurant_setting: Design-led dining room with dramatic bar
sources: Michelin Guide; Time Out Chicago; Robb Report; Chicago Magazine; The Infatuation

description: Girl & The Goat is the most celebrated chef-driven dining destination in the West Loop — a 122-seat restaurant from James Beard Award-winning chef Stephanie Izard (Top Chef Season 4 winner) that helped define the Randolph Restaurant Corridor and remains one of the most consistently praised and most sought-after reservations in Chicago. The family-style menu of bold, globally influenced small plates — divided into meat, fish, and vegetable categories — has been named among the Top 100 Restaurants in the Country by OpenTable and a Chicago Tribune 3-star restaurant. The lively, open-energy dining room and accessible price point combine with serious culinary ambition to make this a true alternative to more formal fine dining.
highlights: James Beard Award-winning chef Stephanie Izard; West Loop; original Randolph Corridor restaurant; family-style globally influenced menu; James Beard nominee Best New Restaurant; OpenTable Top 100.
sources: Michelin Guide; James Beard Foundation; Chicago Tribune; OpenTable; Time Out Chicago; The Infatuation

description: Indienne is the most important Indian restaurant in Chicago's modern history — a one-Michelin-starred River North destination that became the first Indian restaurant to earn a star in Illinois, from a chef-team that brings fine-dining technique and Michelin-level presentation to the extraordinary breadth and depth of Indian regional cuisine. The tasting menu format — rare for Indian cuisine — and the elegant, considered dining room establish an entirely new paradigm for how Indian food can be experienced by an international fine-dining audience.
highlights: One Michelin star; first Indian restaurant to earn a star in Illinois; River North; fine-dining tasting menu format; sets a new standard for Indian cuisine in America; elegant dining room.
sources: Michelin Guide USA 2025; Eater Chicago; Chicago Tribune; Time Out Chicago

description: Next is one of the most conceptually adventurous restaurants in the world — a one-Michelin-starred West Loop tasting menu experience from Grant Achatz's Alinea Group that changes its entire theme, cuisine, décor, and price point with each new menu, taking guests on a journey to a different time, place, or culinary concept every few months. Themes have ranged from 'Paris 1906' to 'The Hunt' to 'Bocuse' to 'Chinese,' and the pre-paid ticketing system has become one of the most discussed models in contemporary restaurant economics. The Alinea Group's most inventive format after Alinea itself, Next is a one-of-a-kind dining concept with no equivalent anywhere.
highlights: One Michelin star; Grant Achatz's Alinea Group; West Loop; completely rotating theme-based tasting menu every few months; pre-paid ticketing model; unique global concept with no equivalent.
restaurant_setting: Design-led dining room with rotating theme
sources: Michelin Guide USA 2025; Condé Nast Traveler; Time Out Chicago; Eater Chicago; Chicago Tribune

description: Esmé is one of Chicago's most artistically ambitious Michelin-starred restaurants — a one-star Lincoln Park dining room from chef Jenner Tomaska and Katrina Bravo where each tasting menu is built in collaboration with a visual artist, and prints of the commissioned work are displayed in the dining room alongside the relevant courses. The result is a dining experience of unusual conceptual depth, where culinary and visual art are inseparable. Tomaska's menu highlights — a milk and pine palate cleanser, Nordic and Japanese spirit cocktails, precisely executed proteins — combine extraordinary technical skill with a genuine creative intelligence.
highlights: One Michelin star; chef Jenner Tomaska; Lincoln Park; artist collaboration for each menu; art displayed in dining room; Nordic and Japanese spirit cocktail programme; dinner begins with cocktails in the lounge.
sources: Michelin Guide USA 2025; Modern Luxury Chicago; Time Out Chicago; Eater Chicago

description: Galit is Chicago's most acclaimed Middle Eastern fine dining restaurant — a one-Michelin-starred Lincoln Park destination from chef Zachary Engel that has become one of the city's most genuinely exciting new additions since its opening in 2019. The menu channels the flavours of Israel, Lebanon, and the broader Levant through a fine-dining sensibility of seasonal produce, exceptional technique, and a wood-fired oven that gives everything a smoky, aromatic depth. The hummus and mezze alone have been called some of the best in America, and the service culture — warm, knowledgeable, and unhurried — matches the quality of the food.
highlights: One Michelin star; chef Zachary Engel; Lincoln Park; Levantine and Middle Eastern fine dining; wood-fired oven; James Beard-nominated; among the best hummus and mezze in the country.
sources: Michelin Guide USA 2025; Eater Chicago; Chicago Tribune; Time Out Chicago

description: Cariño is Chicago's most exciting Michelin-starred newcomer — a one-star Uptown restaurant from chef Norman Fenton that is the most ambitious and personal celebration of Mexican cuisine to open in the city in years, set beneath the rumble of the elevated train in an intimate tasting menu format. Fenton lists ingredients like an auctioneer and his pace is electric, delivering huitlacoche ravioli with fried corn silk, lamb tartare tostada, and chips and salsa as salsa verde jelly with tortilla crumble — dishes that are simultaneously rooted in Mexican culinary tradition and entirely original in their expression.
highlights: One Michelin star; chef Norman Fenton; Uptown; contemporary Mexican tasting menu; huitlacoche ravioli; lamb tartare tostada; under the elevated train; the most personal Mexican fine dining in Chicago.
sources: Michelin Guide USA 2025; Chicago Tribune; Time Out Chicago; Eater Chicago

description: Mako is Chicago's premier omakase destination — a one-Michelin-starred West Loop sushi counter from chef B.K. Park that offers one of the most rigorous and transportive Japanese dining experiences in the Midwest. The intimate counter format, exceptional sourcing of Japanese fish and premium ingredients, and Park's commitment to classical Japanese technique make Mako a restaurant that competes credibly with top omakase destinations in New York and Los Angeles. Among the most sought-after reservations in Chicago, with a regulars-first culture that rewards repeated visits.
highlights: One Michelin star; chef B.K. Park; West Loop omakase counter; among the best Japanese dining in the Midwest; classical Japanese technique; intimate format; most sought-after reservation in Chicago's Japanese scene.
sources: Michelin Guide USA 2025; Chicago Tribune; Time Out Chicago; Eater Chicago

description: Feld is Chicago's most exciting new Michelin-starred restaurant — a one-star, Green Star-awarded Ukrainian Village dining room from chef Jake Potashnick that opened in 2024 and earned a first Michelin star in 2025, making it the most compelling newcomer story in Chicago's recent fine dining history. The 'relationship-to-table' ethos places direct producer relationships at the heart of everything, and the tasting menu is driven entirely by the best the season and Potashnick's network of farmers, fishermen, and foragers can provide. The Green Star alongside the regular star recognises exceptional sustainability practices that are embedded in the concept from the ground up.
highlights: One Michelin star; Green Star 2025; chef Jake Potashnick; Ukrainian Village; relationship-to-table ethos; opened 2024; most admired Chicago debut of recent years; sustainability at the core.
sources: Michelin Guide USA 2025; Eater Chicago; Modern Luxury Chicago; Time Out Chicago

description: Moody Tongue is one of the world's most singular restaurant concepts — a one-Michelin-starred South Loop dining room that occupies the ground floor of what was formerly the world's only two-Michelin-star brewery, offering a 12-to-15-course tasting menu from chef James Bingham in an intimate 28-seat room, with each course paired with a house-brewed beer as well as wine. The brewing programme's integration into every aspect of the menu creates a dining experience with a genuinely original flavour logic, and the La Liste ranking reflects a culinary pedigree that has consistently impressed even the most classically-oriented critics.
highlights: One Michelin star; La Liste; world's former two-Michelin-star brewery; chef James Bingham; South Loop; 28-seat dining room; beer and wine paired tasting menu; unique brewery-to-table concept.
sources: Michelin Guide USA 2025; La Liste; Time Out Chicago; Chicago Tribune; Condé Nast Traveler

description: Atelier is one of Chicago's most quietly brilliant Michelin-starred restaurants — a one-star Lincoln Square dining room from chef Jenner Tomaska (before his current chapter) and co-owners that occupies a minimalist, gallery-like space where a high-concept tasting menu blurs the boundaries between art and cuisine. The well-lit, airy room with its precise geometry evokes an art gallery in the truest sense, and the menu — creative, beautifully calibrated, and deeply personal — has established Atelier as one of the most distinctive Michelin addresses in Chicago's fine dining landscape.
highlights: One Michelin star; Lincoln Square; art gallery-inspired minimalist dining room; high-concept tasting menu; blurs the boundaries between art and food; one of Chicago's most distinctive Michelin addresses.
sources: Michelin Guide USA 2025; Time Out Chicago; Chicago Tribune; Eater Chicago
